ksa_text: Knowledge of computing fundamentals relevant to data and AI work, including computer architecture (processor architectures and instruction sets, memory hierarchy and cache behavior, storage systems and device characteristics), operating system principles, hardware-software interaction, and how infrastructure constraints affect data processing performance and system design decisions.

dcwf.top_element_label: Knowledge of basic physical computer components and architectures, including the functions of various components and peripherals (e.g., CPUs, Network Interface Cards, data storage).

nice.top_element_label: Knowledge of computer architecture principles and practices

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

top_anchors.element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.
Knowledge of basic physical computer components and architectures, including the functions of various components and peripherals (e.g., CPUs, Network Interface Cards, data storage).

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: KSA TF-K-014 (TF, Knowledge) maps to Geospatial Data Analyst scope as moderate fit. Composite score 0.4820 clears the moderate fit threshold of 0.43. Top framework anchors: nist_ai_rmf=0.482, eu_ai_act=0.475, dcwf=0.421. Seniority fit: appropriate; content reflects IC-to-senior-IC execution work within geospatial data analysis, GIS, and location intelligence.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: P2 include upheld. Score 0.4820 for moderate fit domain is consistent with ANL IC-level scope. No internal contradictions identified.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: External validation: does KSA TF-K-014 (TF, composite 0.4820) appear in published data analyst / BI role profiles and workforce frameworks?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Survey of O*NET 15-2051.00 tools/technology section; DCWF technical foundations competencies for analytics work roles does not surface this TF-domain content as a core or common requirement for IC-to-senior-IC data analyst roles. Score 0.4820 may reflect incidental framework overlap. Without direct analytics-domain text confirmation, external ground truth does not support inclusion at this signal level.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: P3 include overturned. Insufficient external evidence for TF domain content at composite 0.4820 without confirming text signals. Revised to exclude.

decision_rationale: Single adversarial overturn by P4 external ground truth. Final decision: exclude. Composite 0.4820, domain TF. Medium confidence — one agent challenged and overturned the preliminary assessment.
